pattern: Copying a structure that can reach itself again needs a memo from original to copy — create each clone once, look it up on every later visit, and cycles take care of themselves.
hints: A plain recursive copy loops forever the moment the graph has a cycle — and every undirected edge is one.
Keep a map from each original node to its clone, created the first time you meet the node.
Traverse with BFS or DFS; for every neighbor, reuse the mapped clone if it exists, otherwise create and enqueue it.

edge_inputs.description: square of four mutually paired vertices
two vertices joined by one edge
star: one hub connected to three leaves
complete graph where every miss in the memo causes infinite recursion

stress.description: random connected undirected graph grown from a spanning tree
